title: Growing skepticism and cost-cutting in the AI industry
summary: Companies and users show disappointment with AI, reduce their use due to costs, and calls for regulation arise amid fears that tech giants will dominate the economy.

title: Norway bans AI in primary schools and studies reveal negative effects on learning
summary: Norway becomes the first country to ban the use of AI in basic education, while research shows that AI inflates grades and harms learning.

title: Video game industry news: prices, departures, and lawsuits
summary: GTA VI price announced at up to 120 euros, executives from Epic and Ubisoft leave the industry, and Half-Life 3 rumors resurface with a nod from Valve.

title: Development tool updates: Bevy, Mesa, TypeScript, and Deno
summary: New versions of Bevy, Mesa with DLSS support, TypeScript 7.0 (faster), and Deno Desktop arrive to improve software development.

title: Increase in scams and cybercrimes in Asia-Pacific and the World Cup
summary: INTERPOL and reports warn about the increase in phishing scams, ransomware, and AI in the Asia-Pacific region, including scams related to the World Cup.

title: Military technology: missiles, aircraft carriers, drones, and laser defense
summary: Advances in Russian nuclear weapons, Chinese manual for attacking aircraft carriers, use of Ukrainian drones for AI, and U.S. preparation with anti-aircraft laser weapons are revealed.

title: Polymarket accused of using fake bets to boost its growth
summary: Polymarket betting platform faces accusations of paying for misleading videos and using fake bets to go viral.

title: Samsung implements ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex, but a bug is detected in Codex
summary: Samsung adopts OpenAI AI tools, while a flaw is discovered in Codex that could write terabytes to SSDs.

title: Sakana AI presents Fugu, an orchestrator of multiple LLMs
summary: Fugu, Sakana AI's new system, combines several language models to match Anthropic's benchmarks.

title: GLM-5.2, the Chinese AI model that catches Silicon Valley's attention
summary: The Chinese open model GLM-5.2 competes with Opus and generates interest in the international community.

title: Cybersecurity incidents: malware, botnets, and leaks in Texas
summary: Canadian intelligence agency cleans infected devices, malware turns routers into proxies, and a leak in Texas exposes 3 million licenses.

title: Privacy and surveillance: Danish activist's record and age verification on Discord
summary: Police search a Danish activist, while Discord tests age verification with wallets and credit cards.

title: China dominates autonomous transportation and strangles critical materials for the US
summary: A report shows Chinese leadership in robotaxis, while China limits the export of strategic materials needed for the US tech industry.

title: New AR features arrive: Rokid Glasses and iOS 27 news
summary: Rokid glasses offer superior pedestrian navigation, while iOS 27 will incorporate practical AI functions integrated into the iPhone.

title: UK bans social media for under-16s
summary: The British government passes a law that prevents children under 16 from accessing social media, and the founder of 'safe TikTok' anticipates that the US will follow suit.

title: 76-year-old woman dies after Tesla crash on autopilot in Texas
summary: A Tesla in autopilot mode crashed into a home in Texas, causing the death of an elderly woman who was inside her house.

title: General Motors lays off 1,000 workers at its electric vehicle plant
summary: GM reduces its workforce at the EV plant and simultaneously incorporates robots to automate production.

title: MSCI assigns SpaceX the lowest possible ESG rating
summary: Elon Musk's space company receives the worst score in environmental, social, and corporate governance criteria due to its impact and practices.

title: Sam Altman's startup Orb investigated for alleged financial irregularities
summary: The eye-scanning company Orb is being subject to an internal investigation for inappropriate financial conduct.

title: Canadian government spent tens of millions on secret contract with Palantir
summary: It is revealed that Canada signed a million-dollar agreement with surveillance company Palantir without public transparency.
